title: Education, political socialization and extremism
abstract: Click to increase image sizeClick to decrease image size Notes 1. It is not the purpose of this paper to discuss conflicting views on how to prevent the specific form of violent extremism called terrorism: the ‘war on terror’ and the Bush doctrine is in sharp contradiction to The Global Action Program to Prevent War and the General Assembly’s Global Counter‐Terrorism Strategy emphasizing that terrorist acts are criminal acts (not acts of war) to be pursued by police and the courts, and that the elimination and reduction of terrorism involves a multi‐faceted approach addressing the political grievances and the root causes that mobilize terror activity as well as delegitimizing terrorist ideology ‘… through the energetic dissemination of moderate views and the promotion of a culture of peace through inter‐faith dialogues …’ (Global Action to Prevent War 2008 Global Action to Prevent War. 2008. 30 program statement. www.globalactionpw.org. [Google Scholar], 3).
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
